item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The "Extradimensional anomaly issues the Foundation a C&D" ending is all too common and all too boring at this point, I'm afraid. This especially doesn't fit for the anomaly sending the letter — Oneiroi looses so much flavor when it is forced through the "corporate jargon" filter that it's unrecognizable from everything good about the GoI.

There's also a big gap between the description — steals all dreams and reduces nightmares — and the test log — where the D-Class not only has nightmares, but actually gets worse.

Test A - ██/█/████

Also, why even have this line? There's only one test and the date is redacted, so it doesn't add anything. There's no information whatsoever, and it doesn't even create an impression of verisimilitude. You'd lose nothing dropping this line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These are my feelings precisely. Boiling Oneiroi down to "dream business" does it no favors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty much just a mythological creature written up reasonably straight, with some GoI stuff tacked on. That, and I really don't like this portrayal of Oneiroi for the same reason I don't like the Fifth Church being portrayed as a straightforward scam: it pigeonholes Oneiroi into a much less interesting position than leaving the nature of Oneiroi ambiguous. That, and 'weird business' is a niche that's been hammered into the ground enough times that I really don't see 'Oneiroi Ltd.' adding anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a tiny mammoth… OF DOOM. Thus, upvoted.

Since somebody mentioned feeling the Oneiroi thing is extraneous, I have to mention that without it I'd just have thought that SCP-2245-1 was just eating psychic goop from people. I wouldn't have known that this article would be crosslinking to Oneiroi otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I know basically nothing about any of the folklore that went into creating this SCP, I still found it very interesting. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Helsinki called, they want their Drowzee back. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If Baku are holy creatures, does that make oneiroi demonic?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel Oneroi feels rather extraneous here. It's much better if you just leave the implications, IMO/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha. Not bad. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I recognized it as a baku from seeing the entry for said creature in the third Pathfinder bestiary. Drowzee only occurred to me after I looked it up to be sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For comparison.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Baku of Monster Rancher is big, dumb, and goofy-looking. Like a gigantic slurpy dog.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: they do have people like that, yas

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually didn't know Drowzee existed until after I was working on this, but both 2245 and Drowzee are based on the mythological baku. However the creepy line still stands, given that the baku of legend is huggable, cute, and benevolent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Drowzee used Dream Eater? Can you actually get away with an SCP based on…

Day 7: D-4356 reported that in all dreams experienced contained a prominent character or characters showing signs of unease or agitation.

Oh, yes you can. Now it's creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It still gets one D-Class a night, just like before, as that seems to only minimally tick off the Collective.

+ SPOILERS
- hide block

It could be seen as 2245 stealing customers, but remember what baku (baki? bakus?) do in the myths, 2245 is eating the Oneiroi delivery people. As for the QPSI test, that is a combination of worldbuilding and giving a hint as to what is happening, the SCP is metabolizing the inspiration that the Oneiroi collective seems intent on spreading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this take on Oneiroi (I think multiple interpretations of a dream-based GOI isn't a bad thing at all), and it went in a much better direction than you had it go before. I myself am not a fan of the QPSI test still and really would advise you get rid of it personally, but other than that, I like this. Sorry I couldn't review your draft in chat when you recently brought it up.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally still object to the QPSI test (what action the creature would have caused to result in researching its "problem-solving ability"), but I am fairly pleased that you have brought the Baku to the table.

From how I see it, 2245 is "stealing customers" from the Oneiroi and they don't like it. I am fairly intrigued with your take on this GoI, since most Oneiroi SCPs are cast as products used by certain parties and this brought a different perspective to the table.

However, I have a question: If experimentation is ceased, does that mean SCP-2245 will not receive dreams anymore? Will it be starving (You could always IV drip nutrients, I guess)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to think that they tend to be somewhat more nebulous, but in-universe I like to think they have some more professional ambassadors/PR people. Out-of-universe, their nebulous portrayal in a previous iteration just confused readers.

item.title: Authorial Commentary
item.content:encoded:
A big thank you to Decibelle, MrWrong, Professor Will, Soulless, and Adam Grey for the reviews.

A brief note on the QPSI test: It measures the problem solving ability of non-sapient sentients in place of some kind of IQ test. Yes it is there for a reason, yes you do have to look a little bit hard to find it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really know how I feel about Oneiroi being depicted so clearly as businessmen, but I love Oneiroi. I upvoted.
